observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Prepare the necessary accounting entries for the reclassification of the
completed projects from CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts;
b. Adjust the Due to LGU account for the amount of expended portion of the
fund transferred by the provincial government and return the unexpended
balance, if applicable; and
c. Record the corresponding unrecorded CY 2024 depreciation expense
therefor, as an adjustment to Prior Period Adjustment account and
accumulated depreciation.

2.12 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Accounting and Treasurer’s Office convene, account and determine
the persons responsible to submit the documents needed to facilitate
preparation of accounting entries for the unadjusted reconciling items;
b. The Municipal Accountant prepare the appropriate accounting entries to
correct the CIB, LCCA balance and other affected accounts, upon
submission of the documents required; and
c. The Municipal Accountant make sure that the book reconciling items
determined in the current reconciliation statements/schedules are adjusted
in the following month.

3.9 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee compose of
adequate number of members, to plan and conduct the physical count of
PPEs;
b. The Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer undertake the preliminary
activities prior to the conduct of inventory taking as prescribed under COA
Circular No. 2020-006, such as the retrieval of the last RPCPPE, updating
of PPE schedules/SLs and retrieval of all property records and documents;
c. The Inventory Committee plan/strategize on how to conduct and complete
the physical inventory by preparing a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) to be
submitted to the Auditor prior to the schedule start of physical count; and
d. The Inventory Committee conduct the physical count of PPE in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2020-006 and submit a RPCPPE to the Auditor.

4.9 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the provincial government,
prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the duly certified list
indicating the name of the taxpayers and the amount of CY 2024 RPT/SET
due and collectible and submit the same report annually; and
b. The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary accounting entries to
adjust the RPT/SET receivables in CY 2024, in accordance with the
certified list provided by the Municipal Treasurer.

5.9 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The LDRRM Council submit to the Auditor the complete list of recipients
and/or proof of distribution and accounting of the distributed relief goods
charged to the CY 2024 QRF;
b. Implementing offices assigned in the distribution of relief goods/food
subsidies must ensure proper accounting and documentation from the
receipt up to the distribution of goods to beneficiaries; and
c. The Municipal Accountant require the submission of proof of distribution
of goods to beneficiaries to record purchases as expense, otherwise record
the goods as inventories held for distribution.

6.13 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer submit to the Auditor through the Municipal
Accountant, the DVs and attachments of the unposted checks, otherwise
submit a report indicating the reason/s (per unposted check) why the DVs
cannot be submitted and proofs of transmittal of the DVs and attachments
to the other personnel;
b. The Budget and Accounting office determine and certify if there was a valid
and existing obligation and expenditure processed by their offices in
relation to the unposted checks (for DVs still unsubmitted by the
Treasurer) and submit a joint certification to the Auditor;
c. The Municipal Treasurer not allow physical access of the DVs and its
attachments to personnel from the other offices;
d. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant ensure that the
submitted DV for payment has not been previously paid by formulating
appropriate control procedures; and
e. The Municipal Treasurer submit a monthly report of unsubmitted paid
check disbursements and attachments (with reason/s for non-submission)
to the Municipal Accountant, to ensure that DVs and its attachments are
properly accounted.

7.10 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer in coordination
with the creditor/source agencies, settle the amounts due or return any
unexpended balance of the funds received for projects/programs with
implemented status; and
b. The Municipal Accountant in coordination with the concerned
implementing department/units, verify the status of implementation of the
projects/programs of the fund transfers still recorded in the Due to NGAs,
to facilitate the return of the unexpended balance, if applicable.

6. We recommend that the Management promptly submit the LDRRMP and Reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to ensure compliance and transparency in the management of disaster risk reduction efforts pursuant to Item 6.2 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013, and Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

7. We recommended that the City Accountant agreed to fast-track the segregation of documents in their Office and immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor the 2,664 vouchers and their corresponding supporting documents; thus, supporting the legality and validity of these disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant agreed to fast-track the segregation of documents in their Office and immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor the 2,664 vouchers and their corresponding supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer strictly adhere to the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01 and ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and appropriate.

12. We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer furnish the Accounting Office with a detailed list of allocated vaccines received by the Municipality;
13. For donations received with costs, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record it in the books in accordance with existing regulations, while for donations received without costs, the Municipal Accountant shall recognize the donations at their fair value as at the date of receipt, which may be ascertained by reference to an active market, or by appraisal as provided in Paragraph 97 of the IPSAS No. 23.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) create an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all its Property, Plant, and Equipment, and submit the required RPCPPE to the Auditor. We also recommended that the Inventory to complete all the procedures in this one-time cleansing of PPE account balances including the reconciliation of PPE accounts per accounting records as against the property records to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.

We recommended that the management avoid the grant of additional cash advances unless the previous one has been settled, and strictly observe the rules and regulations in the liquidation of cash advances pursuant to item 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997: otherwise, imposed sanctions by withholding their salaries according to the stated rules and regulations: and We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately or as soon as the purpose has been served.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer furnish the Accounting Office with a detailed list of allocated vaccines received by the Municipality. For donations received with costs, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record it in the books in accordance with existing regulations, while for donations received without costs, the Municipal Accountant shall recognize the donations at their fair value as at the date of receipt, which may be ascertained by reference to an active market, or by appraisal as provided in Paragraph 97 of the IPSAS No. 23.

We recommended that the Management thru the CSWD, LDRRM, and CHO Offices, require that the original copies of the distribution lists of goods and supplies or list of identified barangay or quarantine facility to support the DVs as basis for confirmation and validation of the funds disbursed. We also recommended that the Accounting Office ensure that the required itemized list of goods distributed to recipients/beneficiaries is attached to the DVs for ease of verification.

We recommended that the concerned officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately their unliquidated cash advances specifically those granted on December 31, 2011 and prior, considering that a final demand was already issued pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all cash advances to Officers and Employees are liquidated within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair representation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements.

1.9 We recommended and Management agreed that the Inventory Committee
carry out a comprehensive physical count, reconcile PPE accounts per
property and accounting records, and submit to COA a Report on
Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment reflecting the actual
status of the assets in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.

2.10 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant promptly deduct the outstanding amount from the monthly
salaries of those still in service on the terms agreed upon by the
Municipality and the respective employee.

2.11 Furthermore, for Accountable Officers and employees who are no longer
in service or are already deceased, we recommended that the Municipal
Accountant initiate the process of writing off their accounts in accordance
with the prevailing regulations.

2.12 Henceforth, cash advances should be liquidated within the prescribed
period and closed at year-end, pursuant to Item 5.1 of COA Circular No.
97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

3.9 We recommended and Management agreed that:
3.9.1 The Municipal Accountant, in collaboration with the Municipal
Treasurer, return any unutilized fund to the Source Agencies. This
action should be taken after confirming that the purpose of the fund
transfer has been fulfilled.

3.9.2 Adjustments be made to properly correct entries in the accounts and
reporting standards be exercised in the preparation of the entries.

4.8 We recommended and Management agreed to promptly submit the
LDRRMP and Reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to ensure
compliance and transparency in the management of disaster risk reduction
efforts pursuant to Item 6.2 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated
March 25, 2013, and Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012.

5.7 We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to fast-track the
segregation of documents in their Office and immediately submit to the
Office of the Auditor the 2,664 disbursement vouchers and their
corresponding supporting documents.

6.7 We recommended and Management agreed that/to:
6.7.1 The Municipal Accountant recognize as inventory purchases of
supplies instead of expense;
6.7.2 The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer prepare
and maintain supplies ledger card and stock card, respectively, and
reconcile their records regularly; and
6.7.3 Use the RIS and SSMI for effective inventory management.

7.6 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Planning
and Development Officer strictly adhere to the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No.
2020-01 and ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the
20% DF are well-planned and appropriate.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant reconcile
their records to correct the discrepancy of Cash- Local Treasury account totaling
P2,241,529.11 in the books.

Henceforth, regular quarterly reconciliation required under Section 6.3 of
COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 should be religiously followed to
avoid existence or accumulation of discrepancies and ensure timely correction of
errors if needed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to
complete the conduct of physical inventory of all its PPE and to reconcile the results of
the physical inventory with the accounting records to ascertain the correctness and
existence of the reported balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Agriculture Officer to expedite the use of the P5, 060,037.00
for the five BUB Projects under his supervision so that immediate goal of
the project could be achieved;
b. Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of the Potable
Water Supply System;
c. BUB Focal Person to closely monitor the implementation of all BUB projects
so that the optimum goal of inclusive growth and poverty reduction could be
fully attained; and
d. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately refund/remit
the excess BUB project funds to the source national government agencies to
avoid possible loss or misuse.

We recommended that the fund transfers to the IAs for the implementation of certain
programs or projects in relation to the SEF of Municipality of Hinigaran must be
completely covered with documentary requirements as enumerated under Section
3.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 for its grant to provide basis for monitoring the
proper use of funds.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant makes the necessary adjusting entries
to record the appropriate receivable account corresponding the fund transfers in the
book for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements. Also, proper
recording of fund transfers should be followed.

We recommended that the municipality ensure optimum utilization of the
20% Development Fund through proper planning, prioritization and fast-tracking
the implementation of development projects to afford its constituency the benefits
of enhanced socio-economic development and environmental management
outcomes.

We recommended that only programs/projects/activities embodied in DILG-DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 be funded by 20% Development Fund in
order to achieve its objectives.

We recommended that the Municipality of Hinigaran fully institutionalize
the MDRRM Office by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions required under
Section 12 of R.A. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 to assure implementation of actions and
measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that the Municipal Government of Hinigaran formulate
and implement a comprehensive and integrated LDRRM Plan in accordance with
the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk
reduction in close coordination with the Local Development Councils (LDCs)
taking into consideration the local needs under four aspects: prevention and
mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor must secure prior
approval or authorization from the Sanggunian Bayan (SB) for those
projects/programs/activities described in generic terms or for all those lump-sum
appropriation pursuant to Supreme Court En Banc Decision G.R. No. 175527
dated December 8, 2008 and reiterated in COA Memorandum No. 2010-014 dated
April 22, 2010.

We recommended that the concerned officials and employees liquidate
and/or refund immediately their cash advances pursuant to the provisions of
Section 89 of P.D. 1445, COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, and COA
Circular 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012, to avoid accumulation of unliquidated
cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the concerned officials and employees liquidate
and/or refund immediately their cash advances pursuant to the provisions of
Section 89 of P.D. 1445, COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, and COA
Circular 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012, to avoid accumulation of unliquidated
cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that Accountable Officers must submit complete
supporting documents and secure the approval of the head of the agency before
granting cash advances for salaries, allowances and other subsistence to ascertain the
legality, validity and accuracy of the transactions in compliance to Section 4 (6)
of P.D. 1445 and Section 1.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. We
further recommended that the Budget Officer, in coordination with the Municipal
Accountant strictly adhere to Section 344 of R.A. 7160 and Section 9 of NGAS
Volume 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adopt measures to
facilitate timely submission of disbursement vouchers, payrolls, official receipts,
their corresponding reports, and other documents necessary to conduct a timely
examination and verification of transactions. We further recommended that the
Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure prompt
submission of these reports in compliance to Sec. 100 and 107 of P.D. 1445, Sec.
7.2.1 (a) and (b) of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, and Art. 218
of the Revised Penal Code.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Treasurer /  Property  Officer  should
   prepare and issue ARE to the personnel having custody and control of  the
   property/equipment to ensure the safety, determine the accountability and
   facilitate the accounting thereof.

We recommended that the  Local  Chief  Executive  (LCE)  direct  the
   Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory of  all
   its PPE and to reconcile the results of the physical inventory  with  the
   accounting records to ascertain the  correctness  and  existence  of  the
   reported balances in the financial statements.

We recommended compliance on the following:

       1. The Municipal Treasurer shall  file  a  request  for  disposal  of
          unserviceable properties supported with necessary documents needed
          for technical evaluation and appraisal; and

       2. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer shall also coordinate  with  the
          Municipal   Accountant   for   proper   classification   of    the
          unserviceable items to Other Asset Account.

We recommended that all buildings  and  other  insurable  properties
   owned by the Municipal Government should be insured with GSIS pursuant to
   RA 656, as amended.

We recommended that the concerned officials and employees  liquidate
   and/or refund immediately their cash advances pursuant to the  provisions
   of Section 89 of P.D. 1445, COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10,  1997,
   and COA Circular 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012, to avoid  accumulation
   of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair  presentation  of  the
   relevant accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that cash advances  for  official  travel  outside  the
   Municipality should be authorized by the head  of  the  agency,  in  this
   case, the Municipal Mayor, in accordance to Section 4 (5)  of  P.D.  1445
   and  Section  3  of  Executive  Order  No.  298  dated  March  23,  2004.
   Accountable Officers should also submit complete supporting documents  to
   ascertain the legality, validity and  accuracy  of  the  transactions  in
   compliance to Sections 1.1.1, 1.1.3, and 1.1.4.1 of COA Circular 2012-001
   dated June 14, 2012.


       We also recommend  that  liquidation  of  cash  advances  should  be
   properly  substantiated  with  the  necessary  supporting  documents   in
   compliance to Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 and Sections 1.2.1 and 1.2.3  of
   COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the cash advances involving large amounts should
   be granted to accountable officers with higher approved amount  of  bond,
   thus higher maximum  cash  accountability,  or  increase  their  approved
   amount of bond in accordance with Section 305 (f) of R.A.  7160,  Section
   7.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, and Section I  of
   Annex C of Treasury Circular No. 02 – 009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor must secure  prior  approval  or
   authorization     from     the     local     sanggunian     for     those
   projects/programs/activities described in generic terms or for all  those
   lump-sum appropriation pursuant to Supreme Court En  Banc  Decision  G.R.
   No. 175527 dated December 8, 2008 and reiterated in  COA  Memorandum  No.
   2010-014 dated April 22, 2010.

We recommended that only  programs/projects/activities  embodied  in
   DILG-DBM  Joint  Memorandum  Circular  No.  2011-1  be  funded   by   20%
   Development Fund in order to achieve its objectives.


       We  further  recommend  that  the  Municipality  enforce  the   full
   implementation of the programs, projects and activities embodied  in  the
   annual development plan so that a positive impact on a wider scale can be
   felt in the locality.

We recommend that the Municipal  Accountant  ensures  that  required
   documentary requirements are duly attached to the  disbursement  vouchers
   before approving any release  of  fund  transfers  to  various  barangays
   pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,  2012
   and Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that cash advances  for  official  travel  should  be
   authorized by the head of the agency, in this case, the Municipal  Mayor,
   in accordance to Section 4 (5) of P.D. 1445. Accountable Officers  should
   also submit complete supporting  documents  to  ascertain  the  legality,
   validity and accuracy of  the  transactions  in  compliance  to  Sections
   1.1.1, 1.1.3, and 1.1.4.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June  14,  2012.
   Furthermore, we recommend that adjusting entries be made to  correct  the
   erroneous recording of students’ subsistence.


       We also recommended that liquidation  of  cash  advances  should  be
   properly  substantiated  with  the  necessary  supporting  documents   in
   compliance to Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 and Sections 1.2.1,  1.2.3,  and
   1.2.4.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

       Stop the practice of splitting purchase  requisitions  or  contracts
   and other related transactions pursuant to Section 54 of the Revised  IRR
   of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 76-41 to promote effective
   competition and transparency in the procurement process and obtain a fair
   and equitable contract for the best interest of the government.

We recommended  that  all  purchase  orders,  regardless  of  amount
   together with its supporting documents shall within five (5) working days
   from issuance thereof, be submitted to the Auditor  pursuant  to  Section
   3.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
